title: Algar's Central Coast
content: Algar Central Coast#A regional map of Algar’s central coast, showing the lands, settlements, and geography familiar to anyone who has grown up in this part of the world.

title: Bridgeport
content:
Bridgeport#Bridgeport is a freehold city of approximately 8,000 souls situated at the mouth of the Godsfall River where it meets Pell’s Harbor, on the western coast of Algar. It is a city of commerce, ambition, and barely contained chaos — a place where fortunes are made and lost, where every faction has a foothold, and where the streets themselves seem to have opinions.
Governance#Bridgeport is governed by the Council of Trades, a body of merchant representatives whose authority derives from coin rather than crown. The current High Convenor is Seraphina Velcross, a sharp political mind who maintains an uneasy balance between the city’s many competing interests.
The Crown maintains a garrison at Fort Royal, commanded by Colonel Alastair Thorneveil. The garrison’s presence is a reminder that Bridgeport’s independence has limits — though most days, the Colonel and the Council maintain a careful mutual tolerance.
Districts & Landmarks#Saltstone Square#The beating heart of Bridgeport’s street life, Saltstone Square is a crowded market district where merchants, laborers, and trouble all congregate. The Strangled Goat tavern sits on its edge — a reliable place for a drink, a rumor, or an ill-advised conversation.
Riverbend Mills#The industrial district along Stonewash Creek, where the city’s grain is ground and its goods are processed. Not glamorous, but essential.
Northwood Stands#The dense woodland that borders Bridgeport to the north. Few go in without good reason, and not all who do come back.
Faith#Three temples serve Bridgeport’s spiritual needs — or at least its official ones:
Temple of Radiant Light — devoted to Aard Shrine of Iron Oath — devoted to Kelleth Umbral House — devoted to Noctis Atmosphere#Bridgeport smells like salt, fish, and ambition. Its streets are loud, its politics are complicated, and its people are pragmatic above all else. It is not a safe city, but it is an interesting one — and for those with the nerve to navigate it, full of opportunity.

title: The World
content:
The World#The Moonshroud Realms is a world of ancient power and creeping decay — where the bones of civilization rest on foundations older than memory, and where something beneath the surface has begun, slowly and inexorably, to come apart.
The Shape of the World#The known world centers on Elderland, a vast continent of diverse peoples, competing kingdoms, and deep wilderness. Within Elderland lies Algar, a country of merchant cities, old forests, and Crown authority that grows thinner the further one travels from the capital.
On Algar’s western coast, where the Godsfall River meets Pell’s Harbor, sits Bridgeport — a freehold city of some 8,000 souls, and the place where this story begins.
The Peoples of the Realms#The Moonshroud Realms is home to many peoples. Among those the party has encountered:
The Aelfaen — a people with deep ties to the old forests, found in great numbers in the woodland settlements south of Bridgeport The Ursinor — bear-folk of the deep woodlands, organized by clan and governed by ancient tradition Humans, who are everywhere, in every role, at every level of society The Feeling of the World#This is not a world of clear heroes and obvious villains. Power is held by those who can keep it, justice is local and imperfect, and the old stories are not always wrong. Magic is real but not commonplace — it draws attention, carries cost, and is rarely as simple as it appears.
Something is wrong with the world at a fundamental level. The party may not know this yet. But they will.
Known Locations#Bridgeport — Freehold city at the mouth of the Godsfall River This page will grow as the party’s knowledge of the world expands.

content: West Bridgeport City Map#A map of West Bridgeport, the freehold city at the mouth of the Godsfall River.

title: Wexford
content:
Wexford#Wexford is a walled woodland town of approximately 1,000 souls, situated some fifty miles south of Bridgeport in the depths of the Wexwood Forest. It sits at the confluence of the Wex River and Spring Creek, and is predominantly home to the Aelfaen — though travellers, traders, and those with business in the forest pass through regularly enough that outsiders are not unknown.
It is a quieter place than Bridgeport, but quieter does not mean simple.
Governance#Wexford operates under a layered authority that keeps several interests in careful balance:
Seneschal Maereth Ilthandor represents the Crown and governs from Sands House Mayor Thomas Greenweald handles civic matters and speaks for the townspeople A Council of Six advises on local affairs The Crown garrison is quartered at the Crown Barracks, commanded by Captain Aurix Valemor — a small but professional force whose presence reminds visitors that Wexford, like Bridgeport, exists within the reach of Algar’s authority.
Key Locations#Inns & Taverns#The Limping Fiddler — the town’s primary inn, run by Elowen Thistledown The Gilded Alder — a more upscale establishment for those with coin to spare Institutions#The Guild Hall — center of Wexford’s commercial life Camber House — local seat of the R.A. Society The Copper Hospice — a place of healing run by Matron Ilyra Fencrest, though there is more to it than meets the eye Feyward Spire — an ancient structure whose purpose is not entirely clear Waywarden Beacon — a landmark for travellers navigating the Wexwood Temples#Marble Canon — temple of Absot, overseen by Hierophant Halwen Marr Lantern-Keeper Temple — devoted to Mollo The Wexwood#The forest surrounding Wexford is old — older than the town, older than the roads, possibly older than memory. The Aelfaen who live here have a relationship with it that outsiders rarely fully understand. It is not hostile, exactly. But it is watchful.
Travellers are advised to stay on marked paths.
Notes#Wexford has not yet been visited by the party. This entry will expand as they learn more.
